title: St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai

heroImage.alt: St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai is a much-loved spiritual town in Tamil Nadu, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai[1] is a Roman Catholic Church located in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. St. Antony's Church, located right in the middle of the Chemmanvilai village, is an important worship center that plays an important role in the lives of the local people.

topPlaces.description: St. Sebastian Church, Madathattuvilai is a Catholic Church located at Kanyakumari, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Madathattuvilai
topPlaces.description: Madathattuvilai is a village under Villukuri Panchayat in Kanyakumari district, the southernmost district in the state of Tamil Nadu in India. 400+ residents of Madathattuvilai have donated their eyes in last one decade and making it a trailblazer among cities and towns across the country.

topPlaces.name: Mandaikadu
topPlaces.description: Mandaikadu is a coastal panchayat town in Kanniyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The town is known for its ten day long annual Koda festival that attracts hundreds of devotees.

topPlaces.name: Mangavilai
topPlaces.description: Mangavilai is a village in Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, India. It is administered under Dharmapuram Panchayat, and is located 9 km away from Nagercoil, the district headquarters of Kanyakumari.

topPlaces.name: Kandanvilai
topPlaces.description: Kandanvilai (கண்டன்விளை) is a village in Kanyakumari District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Puliyoorkurichi
topPlaces.description: Puliyoorkurichi (Tamil: புலியூர்குறிச்சி, also transliterated as Puliyurkurichi or Puliurkuruchi) is a small village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a suburb of Padmanabhapuram, about 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) southeast of Thuckalay on the Trivandrum-Nagercoil Highway.

topPlaces.name: Padmanabhapuram
topPlaces.description: Padmanabhapuram (Malayalam: [pɐd̪mɐnaːbʰɐpuɾɐm]) is a town near Thuckalay in Kanyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As of 2011, the town had a population of 21,342.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (54 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (52 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai?
faq.a: Stays in St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Antony's Church, Chemmanvilai?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chemmanvilai, St. Sebastian Church, Madathattuvilai, Madathattuvilai, Mandaikadu, Mangavilai.
